risk_if_not_implemented: Without Managing Changes To Third-Party Services, third-party risks may go unmanaged, creating supply-chain exposure that compromises the organization.

compensating_control_1.name: Change Management Program
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of a change management program.
compensating_control_1.justification: Change Management Program (CHG-01) provides policy-level governance that compensates for the absence of Managing Changes To Third-Party Services (TPM-10) by establishing documented expectations, accountability structures, and organizational guardrails. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Review of Third-Party Services
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to monitor, regularly review and assess External Service Providers (ESPs) for compliance with established contractual requirements for security, compliance and resilience controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Review of Third-Party Services (TPM-08) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of Managing Changes To Third-Party Services (TPM-10) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
